title: mentha viridis var. lejeuneana
intro: Mentha viridis var. lejeuneana, also known as lemon balm, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family and characterized by its light green or yellowish-green leaves, which emit a sweet, citrusy fragrance. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and mild sedative, making it effective in soothing digestive issues and promoting relaxation. The health benefits of lemon balm include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and menopausal hot flashes. Lemon balm can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to promote overall well-being.
